nodes.label: AI as collaborative conversational partner
nodes.grounding_span: it is just someone (something?) to bounce ideas off, ask questions, etc.

nodes.label: AI as knowledge resource for animal behavior
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to ask questions about pet behaviors, training, health, etc.

nodes.label: AI for client communication
nodes.grounding_span: I had a convo about how to discuss with my clients without hurting their feelings or offending them.

nodes.label: AI as companion in solitary work
nodes.grounding_span: Petsitting can be a rather solitary business, and I don't always have a human around that knows things about animal behavior

nodes.label: Professional competence and client satisfaction
nodes.grounding_span: I got insights as to how to bring up the situation with my client, ideas on what to say and how to say it, etc.

nodes.label: Personal learning and curiosity
nodes.grounding_span: I am learning more about animal behavior than I would have if I did not have AI.

nodes.label: Autonomy and personal control
nodes.grounding_span: I am my own boss and I am free to do what I wish

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: That was very helpful and satisfying.

nodes.label: Indifference to workplace AI pressure
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think it will change too much. I don't really see how this could evolve much past the point I am now

nodes.label: Ongoing conversational exploration — develops ideas through dialogue
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to have ongoing conversations as ideas develop.

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I don't have to have it, I just personally find it helpful.
